preview: Mexico's compact defensive structure and quick counter-attacking transitions will test England's build-up play, with the midfield battle between Mexico's pressing intensity and England's technical superiority determining whether the Three Lions can create enough space for their attacking threats. Both teams enter as regional powers with knockout-stage experience, but England's deeper squad and possession dominance suggest they hold the edge to reach the quarterfinals, provided they avoid the sluggish starts that have plagued them in previous tournaments.
